mission.title: Install and Configure Ingress NGINX Controller
mission.description: The Ingress NGINX Controller is a Kubernetes Ingress controller that uses NGINX as a reverse proxy and load balancer. It is suitable for managing external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster. However, it is important to note that this project is retiring, and users are encouraged to migrate to a Gateway API implementation.

steps.title: Add the Ingress NGINX Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Ingress NGINX Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add ingress-nginx https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/main/deploy/helm-chart/ingress-nginx

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.

steps.title: Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
steps.description:
This command installs the Ingress NGINX Controller in the 'ingress-nginx' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace --version 4.10.1

```

> Ensure you are using the correct version of the Helm chart.

steps.title: Create an Ingress resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Ingress resource to demonstrate how to route traffic to your services.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: ingress-nginx
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.local' and 'example-service' with your actual service details.

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF

EOF

steps.title: Verify the Ingress Controller is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Ingress NGINX Controller pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> Look for the 'Running' status in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Ingress NGINX Controller
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Ingress NGINX Controller and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Ingress resources and any associated configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx

kubectl delete namespace ingress-nginx

```

> This will remove the Ingress NGINX installation.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Ingress resources. This step will upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get ingress -n ingress-nginx -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --version 4.10.1

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> Backup your existing Ingress resources.
> Upgrade to the specified version of the Helm chart.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get ingress -n ingress-nginx -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Ingress" in version "networking.k8s.io/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the Kubernetes cluster version is lower than 1.19, which does not support the networking.k8s.io/v1 API.

**Fix:**
Upgrade your Kubernetes cluster to version 1.19 or higher.

**Affected versions:** Kubernetes <= 1.18

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service "example-service" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service specified in the Ingress resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service name is correct and that it exists in the same namespace as the Ingress resource.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not routing traffic as expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ingress resource is misconfigured or if the NGINX Controller is not properly deployed.

**Fix:**
Check the Ingress resource configuration and ensure the NGINX Controller pods are running without errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the request to the Kubernetes API server timed out, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and ensure the Kubernetes API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom NGINX templates and improved health checks.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy annotations for configuration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your Ingress resources to use the new configuration options as per the latest documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Ingress resource listed with the command 'kubectl get ingress -n ingress-nginx' and the status should indicate it is ready.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
